Category: Momentum, Asset Class Picking, Rotational System, Market Timing, Fund Picking
Strategy Description: A rotational strategy that switches between two negatively correlated assets (e.g., stocks and bonds) based on their relative performance over a recent period.
Core Logic: Exploits the negative correlation between asset classes by holding the one with the strongest recent momentum, aiming to improve risk-adjusted returns over a static mix.
Entry Conditions: Compare the performance of two assets (e.g., VFINX equity fund and VUSTX bond fund) over the prior quarter. Buy the asset with the higher return.
Exit Conditions: Hold for one quarter and repeat the comparison.
Risk Management Rules: Quarterly rebalancing; rotation into defensive assets during equity stress.
Market Regime Suitability: Partially suitable as a hedge; tactical allocation rotates out of equities during stress.
Full Notes: Simple criteria for switching can lead to lower volatility without significant return penalties. Works well with passive ETFs.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Negative correlation, Relative pricing, Relative volatility, Trend following.
